content.text:
Based on the chart, I need to find the meat (red dot) with the lowest cost per 100 calories (lowest Y-axis value), since that maximizes calories per dollar spent.

<think>
The Y-axis shows Cost (USD) per 100 calories. To maximize calories with $5, I want the meat with the lowest Y value.

Looking at the red (Meats) dots in the chart from lowest to highest Y:
- **Pork belly**: ~$0.40/100 cal at ~4 cal/g (lower right area)
- **80% ground beef**: ~$0.40/100 cal at ~3.5 cal/g
- **Chicken wing**: ~$0.42/100 cal at ~2.5 cal/g
- **Turkey**: ~$0.60/100 cal
- **80% ground lamb**: ~$0.50/100 cal
- Others are higher

Pork belly appears to be positioned at the lowest point on the Y-axis among all meats, at approximately $0.40 per 100 calories. With $5, Bob could get roughly 5/0.40 × 100 = **1,250 calories** from pork belly.

This is the best value among meats for maximizing caloric intake per dollar.
</think>

<answer>
Pork belly. Among all meats in the chart, pork belly has the lowest cost per 100 calories (approximately $0.40/100 cal), meaning $5 buys the most calories (~1,250 calories) compared to any other meat option.
</answer>
